title: Server Health Audit — Resolved Findings
scope: server/ workspace — 90,663 LOC Rust, 92 endpoints, 8 crates
methodology: Automated agent sweep across 6 dimensions: handlers, errors, types/DB, auth/security, testing, deps/config. Cross-referenced against Architecture.md, RUST_STYLE_GUIDE.md, and review skill conventions.
strengths: Zero SQL injection vectors — all queries parameterized, format! only interpolates compile-time constants
Zero unsafe code blocks across entire workspace
DatabaseHelper → transform pattern consistently applied across 20+ types; no raw i8 leaks to public structs
Single-table ownership fully enforced — zero sqlx queries in handler files
Session auth: 32-byte CSPRNG split key/secret, blake3 keyed hash, constant-time comparison, bcrypt on blocking threads, timing-attack resistant login via dummy hash
Epoch-based permission invalidation propagates account/permission changes in seconds
Error surfacing: to_http_response() used consistently, Sqlx/DatabaseError/IoError redacted, 94 client-facing variants individually tested
Strong input validation at API boundaries with length caps, format checks, enum validation, FOR UPDATE locks on addon caps
Workflow engine thoroughly tested: pure unit tests + real-DB integration tests including EpochGuard CAS, split atomicity, monitor behavior
306 total tests with real depth in critical areas (sessions, permissions, rate limiter, workflow engine)
Zero hardcoded secrets (dummy bcrypt hash is intentional timing-attack mitigation)

resolved_findings.resolution: Created GitHub Actions workflow: cargo test --workspace, cargo clippy, cargo fmt --check.

resolved_findings.problem: No structured logging — entire server uses println!/eprintln!
resolved_findings.resolution: Added tracing + tracing-subscriber. Replaced all println!/eprintln! with tracing macros. DEC-118.
Panic was in #[test] code, not production.

resolved_findings.problem: Database pool running on sqlx defaults (max 10 connections)
resolved_findings.resolution: Switched to MySqlPoolOptions with configurable DB_MAX_CONNECTIONS / DB_MIN_CONNECTIONS env vars.

resolved_findings.problem: CORS: Authorization header missing from production allowed headers
resolved_findings.resolution: Added AUTHORIZATION to prod allowed headers in header_settings.rs.
DB flag set to Enabled; startup warning logs if disabled; bucket_capacity bug also fixed.

resolved_findings.problem: No HTTP handler tests — 0% coverage at the request/response boundary
resolved_findings.resolution: Replaced with default-deny route architecture + public route sentinel test (EXPECTED_PUBLIC_ROUTE_COUNT).

resolved_findings.problem: TemplateError has Display and std::error::Error commented out
resolved_findings.resolution: Uncommented and verified Display and Error implementations.
Replaced with dotenvy 0.15 in all 3 crates (commit a8d74c8).
rand (UUIDs, share codes, jitter) and actix-rt (spawn_blocking, sweeper intervals, main, Error::JoinError) are production deps. Only insta is test-only.

resolved_findings.problem: reqwest::Client created per email send — no connection reuse
resolved_findings.resolution: Stored reqwest::Client in Postmark struct, created once at startup.

resolved_findings.problem: X-Forwarded-For trust — rate limiter can be bypassed if server is directly exposed
resolved_findings.resolution: DEC-141 banned X-Forwarded-For; extract_client_ip() uses X-Real-IP / X-Real-Client-IP; documented in DEPLOYMENT.md.
DEPLOYMENT.md lines 49-77 document all 23 env vars with descriptions and examples; STARTUP_GATES.md cross-references critical ones with failure modes.
Added Strict-Transport-Security header to DefaultHeaders in api_server.rs; confirmed Railway does not set it at the edge.

resolved_findings.problem: Some handler structs deviate from the logic() convention
resolved_findings.resolution: list/by_id documented as accepted variant in style guide; email verification response() renamed to logic(); ScanSessionPost deferred to later refactor.

metrics_at_audit_time.handler_test_coverage: structural — default-deny route architecture + public route sentinel
